section.title: People to follow
section.intro: Writers and projects that consistently surface information or analysis before it reaches mainstream coverage.

resources.title: Ethan Mollick / One Useful Thing
resources.author: Ethan Mollick
resources.description: Wharton professor. The single most useful person to follow for understanding how AI works in practice. Runs experiments, publishes findings, no hype.

resources.title: Arvind Narayanan / AI as Normal Technology
resources.author: Arvind Narayanan & Sayash Kapoor
resources.description: Princeton. Separates real AI capability from marketing claims. The companion book is in the Foundations section.

resources.title: Simon Willison
resources.description: Documents what AI tools actually do with code, screenshots, and reproducible examples. No hype, no dread.

resources.title: Abeba Birhane
resources.description: Ethiopian-born AI ethics researcher, Mozilla Fellow. Writes about data practices, annotation labor, and AI development from a Global South perspective.

resources.title: Joy Buolamwini / Algorithmic Justice League
resources.description: Facial recognition bias research that changed industry practices. Founded the Algorithmic Justice League. Memoir: "Unmasking AI."

resources.title: Meredith Whittaker
resources.description: Signal president, AI Now Institute co-founder. Focuses on privacy, power, and the structural incentives behind AI deployment.

resources.title: The Convivial Society
resources.author: L.M. Sacasas
resources.description: Technology criticism from the humanistic tradition – Illich, Ellul, Arendt. Focuses on what's lost when labor and cognition are mediated by machines.

resources.title: Maggie Appleton
resources.description: Designer and anthropologist examining how metaphors shape understanding of technology. Visual essays on AI concepts.

resources.title: Chain of Thought
resources.author: Dan Shipper
resources.description: Documents building a business with AI tools – what works, what breaks, what the experience teaches.

resources.title: Don't Worry About the Vase
resources.author: Zvi Mowshowitz
resources.description: Dense AI news curation covering capability, policy, safety, and cultural commentary. Opinionated but transparent about reasoning.

resources.title: DAIR Institute
resources.author: Timnit Gebru
resources.description: AI research independent of corporate and governmental influence. Gebru and Torres developed the TESCREAL framework critiquing the overlapping ideologies driving AI development.

resources.title: Tech Won't Save Us
resources.author: Paris Marx
resources.description: Technology, labor, and power. Interviews labor organizers, policy researchers, and critics who rarely appear in mainstream tech media.

resources.title: Cybernetic Forests
resources.description: Arts-and-criticism-inflected AI commentary. Less frequent, higher signal-to-noise ratio.
